description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Coco Gauff and Elina Svitolina in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 27 2026.

question: Game Handicap: WB (-1.5) vs Rune Eaters (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Winter Bear and Rune Eaters in the EPL Championship Playoffs, scheduled for January 25 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Winter Bear" if Winter Bear wins 2 or more games than Rune Eaters in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Rune Eaters".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Konyaspor vs. Göztepe SK: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming Süper Lig game between Konyaspor and Göztepe SK, scheduled for February 8 at 9:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Konyaspor and Göztepe SK combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on tff.org. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: Map Handicap: HAVU (-1.5) vs BIG (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between HAVU and BIG in the United21 x Esplay Challenge Playoffs, scheduled for January 25 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "HAVU" if HAVU wins 2 or more maps than BIG in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "BIG".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: HAVU vs BIG (BO3) - United21 x Esplay Challenge Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between HAVU and BIG in the United21 x Esplay Challenge Playoffs, scheduled for January 25 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "HAVU" if HAVU win the match against BIG.

This market will resolve to "BIG" if BIG win the match against HAVU.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Map Handicap: PRV (-2.5) vs Team Falcons (+2.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Team Falcons and PARIVISION in the BLAST Bounty Playoffs, scheduled for January 25 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION wins 3 or more maps than Team Falcons in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Team Falcons".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.
